skill.name: skywork-search
skill.description: Search the web with the Skywork API, authenticate with a Skywork token, and save sourced result snippets for later reading.
skill.summary: Runs up to three Skywork web searches and returns saved result files with source URLs.
skill.author: SkyworkAI

security_audit.summary: The skill is a real Skywork web search client, and the network, environment, and browser-launch behavior mostly match that purpose. Publication should wait for fixes because search queries are used directly in output filenames and the authentication token is cached in a hidden home-directory file without explicit permission controls.

high_findings.title: Query-Controlled Output Path Allows File Write Outside Temp Directory
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The search query is used directly in the result filename. A query containing path separators or an absolute path can cause writes outside the temporary output directory.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filename is built from user-controlled query text before opening the output path for writing. No sanitization or basename enforcement is visible at the cited lines.

high_findings.title: Skywork Token Cached Without Explicit File Permission Hardening
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The auth flow stores token data in ~/.skywork_token and writes it with normal open defaults. This can expose a reusable token on systems with permissive umask or shared home access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code and documentation both confirm home-directory token caching. The risk depends on host permissions, but there is no chmod or secure-storage mechanism in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: Environment-Overrideable Endpoints Receive Authentication Data
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: API and web base URLs can be overridden through environment variables, and requests include Skywork authentication tokens. This is useful for testing but risky if untrusted environment variables are present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint overrides and token-bearing requests are directly visible. Legitimate deployment use is possible, so this is not evidence of malicious intent by itself.

medium_findings.title: Browser Launch Uses External Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The auth script launches platform browser commands with a URL derived from configuration and login data. The command names are fixed, but Windows uses shell execution and the URL is not validated.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess usage is confirmed and has a clear purpose. Risk is limited by fixed command arguments, but shell execution and configurable URL input keep it security-relevant.

low_findings.title: Expected Network Access for Search and Authentication
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE_AS_MALICIOUS: Network access is central to this skill. The reviewed calls target Skywork authentication and web search endpoints, and no unrelated exfiltration endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source files and skill documentation describe a web search API client. I did not find evidence of unrelated network destinations in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE_AS_CODE_EXECUTION: The SKILL.md shell detections are fenced usage examples that tell an assistant how to run the bundled scripts. They are not hidden runtime code inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection shows markdown code fences with ordinary usage instructions. The actual executable subprocess behavior is separately evaluated in scripts/skywork_auth.py.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Have No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited weak-cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic algorithm use. They are frontmatter, documentation text, or argparse setup in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection of the exact cited lines found no hash, cipher, or cryptographic primitive. This appears to be a scanner pattern mismatch.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Benign Platform Checks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE_AS_MALICIOUS: The platform check selects an OS-specific browser opener, and token checks support normal authentication flow. No host inventory or data collection behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The platform value only controls browser launch behavior. I did not find evidence of broader system enumeration in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsanitized User Input in Filesystem Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The query argument is interpolated into a filename before writing search results. Sanitize the filename or use generated names that cannot contain path separators.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The argument parser accepts raw query strings, and the same value is used in the output filename. No normalization or validation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Plain Token Cache in Hidden Home File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill stores token data in a hidden file in the user's home directory. Prefer OS credential storage or create the file with owner-only permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token path and write operation are both explicit in the code. The exact file permissions depend on runtime umask, which prevents absolute certainty.

content.user_title: Search Current Web Results with Skywork
content.value_statement: AI assistants need fresh sources for recent facts, market updates, and news. This skill lets Claude, Codex, and Claude Code run Skywork web searches and cite result URLs.
actual_capabilities: Authenticates with Skywork using an environment token, cached token, or browser login flow.
Runs one to three web search queries in a single command.
Sends search requests to the Skywork web search API with a token header.
Saves each query result as a text file in a temporary directory.
Formats results with source URLs and content snippets for later synthesis.
Prints saved result file paths so the assistant can read and cite them.
limitations: The script depends on Skywork API availability and a valid Skywork account token.
Each invocation accepts a maximum of three search queries.
Each query request has a 30-second timeout.
Search quality depends on query wording and the API result set.

use_cases.title: Recent News Briefing
use_cases.description: Find current articles and summarize the main developments with source links.

use_cases.title: Document Source Gathering
use_cases.description: Collect recent facts, statistics, and citations before drafting a report or presentation.

use_cases.title: Technical Update Check
use_cases.description: Look up recent product, library, or platform changes before answering implementation questions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Current Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a user asks for recent information about one topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for current information about [topic]. Summarize the most relevant findings and include source URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Recent Sources
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the answer should compare multiple recent perspectives.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic] from at least two focused angles. Compare the findings and note any source disagreement.

prompt_templates.title: Research for a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before writing a report, memo, article, or presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [topic] for a [format]. Gather recent facts, useful statistics, and source URLs before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Query Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for complex topics that need several targeted searches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Break [research question] into up to three precise search queries. Run them, synthesize the results, and explain source confidence.

output_examples.input: What are the latest developments in quantum computing?
output_examples.output: A concise briefing with recent breakthroughs, company or lab updates, and URLs for each source used.

output_examples.input: Find current statistics for electric vehicle sales in Europe.
output_examples.output: A sourced summary of recent sales figures, publication dates, and notes about regional coverage.

output_examples.input: Research recent changes in a software platform before I write migration notes.
output_examples.output: A short list of relevant release updates, documentation links, and implications for migration planning.

best_practices: Use specific query terms, dates, regions, and product names when possible.
Read the saved result files before summarizing or citing any source.
Tell users when results are sparse, inconsistent, or dependent on one source.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for private data lookups or credential handling beyond Skywork login.
Do not present search snippets as verified facts without source context.
Do not run broad vague queries when a focused query would answer the request.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Skywork account?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs a valid Skywork token from the environment, a cached token, or browser login.

faq.question: How many searches can it run at once?
faq.answer: It accepts one to three query strings per invocation.

faq.question: Where are search results stored?
faq.answer: The script writes text result files into a temporary directory and prints their paths.

faq.question: Can it cite sources?
faq.answer: Yes. Search results include source URLs that the assistant can cite after reading the files.

faq.question: Does it replace full web browsing?
faq.answer: No. It returns API search snippets and URLs, not a complete browser session.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace audit high risk?
faq.answer: The reviewed version has unsafe filename handling and token cache permission concerns that should be fixed before publication.
